---
title: 'RTSL 100 Million Lives R Markdown'
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
author: University of Washington
output: html_document
header-includes:
  # https://github.com/rstudio/rmarkdown/issues/646
  - \usepackage{fvextra}
  - \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
  - \usepackage{float}
  - \floatplacement{figure}{H}
  - \usepackage{xcolor}
  - \usepackage{booktabs}
  - \usepackage{caption} 
geometry: margin = 0.3 in
urlcolor: blue
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE, echo=F}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, error = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
                      fig.height = 4, fig.width = 6, fig.align = "center")
pacman::p_load(tidyverse) # additional packages can be included here
```

```{r prep, message=F, warning=F, echo=F}
# Load required libraries
library(readxl)
library(data.table)
library(knitr)
library(survival)
library(ggplot2)
library(survminer)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(sf)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(rnaturalearthdata)
library(ggbeeswarm)
library(scales)  # for comma()

library(dplyr)
library(kableExtra)
library(DT)
library(plotly)
library(openxlsx)
library(ggrepel)

# Working paths

wd <- "C:/Users/wrgar/OneDrive - UW/02Work/ResolveToSaveLives/100MLives/Github/UW-RTSL-100MLives/"
wd_code <- paste0(wd,"code/")
# Raw data not available on GitHub
#wd_raw <- paste0("C:/Users/wrgar/OneDrive - UW/02Work/ResolveToSaveLives/","100MLives/data/raw/")
wd_raw <- paste0(wd,"data/raw/")

# Processed data (from base rates and tps)
wd_data <- paste0(wd,"data/processed/")
wd_outp <- paste0(wd,"output/")
```

```{r funct, message=F, warning=F, echo=F}
# Function to summarize a continuous variable by group
summarize_dt <- function(dt, group_var, cont_var) {
  dt[, .(
    N    = sum(!is.na(get(cont_var))),
    mean = mean(get(cont_var), na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(get(cont_var), na.rm = TRUE),
    min  = min(get(cont_var), na.rm = TRUE),
    max  = max(get(cont_var), na.rm = TRUE),
    p25  = quantile(get(cont_var), 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    p50  = quantile(get(cont_var), 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),
    p75  = quantile(get(cont_var), 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  ), by = list(get(group_var))]
}



# Full WHO standard population (ages 0–100)
full_who_std <- data.table(
  age = 0:100,
  weight = c(
    rep(0.013, 10),  # 0–9
    rep(0.014, 10),  # 10–19
    rep(0.013, 10),  # 20–29
    rep(0.012, 10),  # 30–39
    rep(0.011, 10),  # 40–49
    rep(0.009, 10),  # 50–59
    rep(0.007, 10),  # 60–69
    rep(0.005, 10),  # 70–79
    rep(0.003, 10),  # 80–89
    rep(0.001, 10),  # 90–99
    0.001            # 100+
  )
)

# Filter for ages 20–94
who_std <- full_who_std[age >= 20 & age <= 94]

# Group 95+ (ages 95–100)
who_95plus <- full_who_std[age >= 95, .(weight = sum(weight))]
who_std <- rbind(who_std, data.table(age = 95, weight = who_95plus$weight))

# Normalize weights so they sum to 1
who_std[, weight := weight / sum(weight)]


calculate_asmr <- function(dt, 
                           std_pop      = who_std, 
                           deaths_col   = "deaths", 
                           population_col = "population",
                           disagg_var   = "sex") {
  # Ensure age is numeric (handles "95+" as 95)
  dt[, age := as.integer(gsub("\\+", "", age))]
  std_pop[, age := as.integer(gsub("\\+", "", age))]
  
  # Merge with standard population weights
  dt <- merge(dt, std_pop, by = "age", all.x = TRUE)
  
  # Calculate age‐specific mortality rate
  dt[, rate := get(deaths_col) / get(population_col)]
  
  # Apply the standard weight
  dt[, weighted_rate := rate * weight]
  
  # Build grouping vector: year + whatever disagg_var is
  group_vars <- c("year", disagg_var)
  
  # Sum up weighted rates and scale to per 100k
  asmr <- dt[, .(ASMR = sum(weighted_rate, na.rm = TRUE) * 1e5), 
             by = group_vars]
  
  return(asmr[])
}

```

```{r output-functions, message=F, warning=F, echo=F}
# Function to group and summarize data

deaths_summary <- function(dt,
                           baseline_label = "b.a.u",
                           by_cols        = c("age_group", "sex", "year",
                                              "location", "cause", "age"),
                           value_col      = "dead") {
  stopifnot(is.data.table(dt))

  ## Number of interventions
  #n_interventions <- length(unique(dt$intervention)) - 1
  n_interventions <- uniqueN(dt[intervention != baseline_label, intervention])
  
  ## 1. baseline totals --------------------------------------------------
  baseline <- dt[intervention == baseline_label,
                 .(baseline_deaths = sum(get(value_col), na.rm = TRUE)),
                 by = by_cols]

  baseline[,c("intervention"):=NULL]
  
  ## 2. non-baseline observations --------------------------------------
  nonbaseline <- dt[intervention != baseline_label]

  nonbaseline <- nonbaseline[,
                 .(deaths_projected = sum(get(value_col), na.rm = TRUE)),
                 by = by_cols]
  
  if (!("intervention" %in% by_cols)) {
  nonbaseline[, deaths_projected := deaths_projected / n_interventions]
  }
  
  ## 3. attach the baseline deaths -------------------------------------
  
  merged <- merge(nonbaseline, baseline,
                    all.x = TRUE,
                  allow.cartesian = TRUE)

  ## 4. deaths avoided / delayed ---------------------------------------
  merged[, deaths_delayed := (baseline_deaths - deaths_projected)]

  ## 5. final summary — *same* by_cols ----------------------------------
  merged[, deaths_delayed_ave := deaths_delayed/24]
  
  merged[, deaths_delayed_pct := deaths_delayed/baseline_deaths]
}
```

# Background
This project aims to answer this research question: At the global scale, what would be the future health impact of implementing Resolve to Save Lives hypertension treatment, statin treatment, sodium reduction, and trans-fat elimination interventions at the global and national levels over a 25 year time horizon (2025-2050)?

```{r project-overview, fig.cap="Causal Diagram", echo=FALSE, out.width="80%"}
knitr::include_graphics("causal_diagram_nodementia.png")
```

```{r transversals, message=F, warning=F, echo=F}
# Read WB Countries Classification
countries_dt <- fread(paste0(wd_raw,"Transversals/Country_groupings_extended.csv"))
countries_dt <- countries_dt[,c("iso3","location_wb","Super_region","SDI"),with=F]
setnames(countries_dt, c("location_wb","Super_region", "SDI"), c("location", "region","income"))

countries_dt[location=="United States of America", location := "United States"]
countries_dt[location=="Egypt, Arab Rep.", location := "Egypt"]
countries_dt[location=="Congo, Dem. Rep.", location := "Democratic Republic of the Congo"]
countries_dt[location=="Congo, Rep.", location := "Congo"]
countries_dt[location=="Yemen, Rep.", location := "Yemen"]
countries_dt[location=="Slovak Republic", location := "Slovakia"]
countries_dt[location=="Venezuela, RB", location := "Venezuela (Bolivarian Republic of)"]
countries_dt[location=="Vietnam", location := "Viet Nam"]
countries_dt[location=="Cote d'Ivoire", location := "Ivory Coast"]
countries_dt[location=="Korea, Rep.", location := "Republic of Korea"]

#Iran, Islamic Rep.
countries_dt[location=="Iran, Islamic Rep.", location := "Iran (Islamic Republic of)"]

countries_dt[location=="Czech Republic", location := "Czechia"]
countries_dt[location=="Bahamas, The", location := "Bahamas"]
countries_dt[location=="Gambia, The", location := "Gambia"]
countries_dt[location=="Lao PDR", location := "Lao People's Democratic Republic"]
countries_dt[location=="Moldova", location := "Republic of Moldova"]
countries_dt[location=="Micronesia, Fed. Sts.", location := "Micronesia (Federated States of)"]  
countries_dt[location=="South Sudan", iso3 := "SDS"]
	

countries_dt$income <- NULL

## Fixing some missing regions

new_countries <- data.table(
  iso3     = c("LCA", "PRK", "VCT", "KGZ", "PSE", "TWN"),
  location = c(
    "Saint Lucia",
    "Democratic People's Republic of Korea",
    "Saint Vincent and the Grenadines",
    "Kyrgyzstan",
    "Palestine",
    "Taiwan (Province of China)"
  ),
  region   = c(
    "Latin America and Caribbean",
    "Southeast Asia, East Asia, and Oceania",
    "Latin America and Caribbean",
    "Central Europe, Eastern Europe, and Central Asia",
    "North Africa and Middle East",
    "Southeast Asia, East Asia, and Oceania"
  )
)

# bind them on
countries_dt <- rbind(countries_dt, new_countries, use.names = TRUE)

# Population counts

# load("wpp.adj.Rda")
# 
# setDT(wpp.adj)
wpp.adj <- as.data.table(readRDS(paste0(wd_data,"UN2024/","PopulationsSingleAge0050.rds")))

pop_world <- wpp.adj[year_id == 2019 & age>=20, sum(Nx, na.rm = TRUE)]
```

# Policy Scenarios

## Hypertension Control

```{r out-policy-bp, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# Blood Pressure control
covfxn <- fread(paste0(wd_data,"covfxn2.csv"))

dt_plot <- covfxn[,c("Country","Year","Business as usual","Progress","Aspirational"),with=F]

dt_plot <- melt(dt_plot, id.vars = c("Country","Year"), variable.name = "intervention", value.name = "scenario_value")

# Spaghetti Plot: All Lines in Grey
g <-ggplot(dt_plot[intervention=="Progress",], aes(x = Year, y = scenario_value*100, group = Country)) +
  geom_line(color = "grey70", size = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(2020, 2050, 5),
    limits = c(2020, 2051),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_color_grey(start = 0.9, end = 0.3) +
  guides(color = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Improved Hypertension Control",
       x = "Year", y = "Hypertension Control Rate (%)") +
theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +  # optional: sets base font size
theme(
  plot.title = element_text(size = 28, face = "bold"),
  axis.title.x = element_text(size = 22, margin = margin(t = 16)),  # more top margin to push label down
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 22, margin = margin(r = 12)),
  axis.text = element_text(size = 20),
  plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 30, l = 10)  # ⬅️ key: increase bottom margin of the plot
)
g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_policy_htnv1.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 6, dpi =300)

# Linear scale up policy
dt_hbp_control <- readRDS(file = paste0(wd_data,"BloodPressure/", "hbp_control_data.rds"))

dt_hbp_control[baseline_ctrl> control_scaled,control_scaled:= baseline_ctrl]

# Spaghetti Plot: All Lines in Grey
g <-ggplot(dt_hbp_control, aes(x = year, y = control_scaled*100, group = location)) +
  geom_line(color = "grey70", size = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(2020, 2050, 5),
    limits = c(2020, 2051),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_color_grey(start = 0.9, end = 0.3) +
  guides(color = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Improved Hypertension Control",
       x = "Year", y = "Hypertension Control Rate (%)") +
theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +  # optional: sets base font size
theme(
  plot.title = element_text(size = 28, face = "bold"),
  axis.title.x = element_text(size = 22, margin = margin(t = 16)),  # more top margin to push label down
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 22, margin = margin(r = 12)),
  axis.text = element_text(size = 20),
  plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 30, l = 10)  # ⬅️ key: increase bottom margin of the plot
)
g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_policy_htn.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 6, dpi =300)

# Data for High Blood Pressure Control and Salt Map

data.in <- fread(paste0(wd,"bp_data6.csv"))%>%rename(location = location_gbd)%>%select(-Year, -Country)
data.in$salt[data.in$location=="China"]<-4.83*2.54


dt_hbp <- data.in[Age.group %in% c("50-54"), c("location","sex","raisedBP","htncov"),with = FALSE]

# Merge with countries data
dt_plot <- merge(dt_hbp, countries_dt, by = "location", all.x = TRUE)

# Get world map as an sf object
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# Merge your data with the world map data (using the ISO3 codes)
world_dt <- merge(world, dt_plot, by.x = "adm0_a3", by.y = "iso3", all.x = TRUE)

# Plot the map with ggplot2
g <- ggplot(world_dt[sex="Female",]) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = raisedBP*100)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    oob      = squish,                # clamp values <0 or >500 back into [0,500]
    na.value = "grey90",
    name     = "(%)",
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title   = "High Blood Pressure (%) 2019. Female",
    caption = "Source: GBD 2021. Age 50 to 54 for reference"
  )

g
ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_map_hbp_female.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)

g <- ggplot(world_dt[sex="Female",]) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = htncov*100)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    oob      = squish,                # clamp values <0 or >500 back into [0,500]
    na.value = "grey90",
    name     = "(%)",
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title   = "Hypertension Control Coverage (%) 2019. Female",
    caption = "Source: GBD 2021. Age 50 to 54 for reference"
  )

g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_map_htncov_female.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)

```

```{r out-policy-bp-past, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}

path_ncd <- "C:/Users/wrgar/OneDrive - UW/02Work/ResolveToSaveLives/100MLives/data/raw/NCD-RisC/"
dt_ncdrisk <- fread(paste0(path_ncd,"NCD-RisC_Lancet_2021_Hypertension_age_standardised_countries.csv"))


setnames(dt_ncdrisk,"Proportion of diagnosed hypertension among all hypertension", "diagnosed")
setnames(dt_ncdrisk,"Proportion of treated hypertension among all hypertension", "treated")
setnames(dt_ncdrisk,"Proportion of controlled hypertension among all hypertension", "controlled")

setnames(dt_ncdrisk,"Country/Region/World", "Country")

cols_to_keep <- c("Country", "ISO", "Sex", "Year", "Age","diagnosed","treated","controlled")
dt_ncdrisk <- dt_ncdrisk[, ..cols_to_keep]

dt <- copy(dt_ncdrisk)
setDT(dt)

setorder(dt, Country, Sex, Year)

# Year-to-year percentage point changes
dt[, `:=`(
    diagnosed_change = 100 * (diagnosed - shift(diagnosed)),
    treated_change   = 100 * (treated   - shift(treated)),
    controlled_change = 100 * (controlled - shift(controlled))
), by = .(Country, Sex)]

dt_change <- dt[!is.na(diagnosed_change)]

# Long format
plot_dt <- melt(
  dt_change,
  id.vars = c("Country", "ISO", "Sex", "Year"),
  measure.vars = c("diagnosed_change", "treated_change", "controlled_change"),
  variable.name = "indicator",
  value.name = "change_pct"
)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# FIXED: expand dataset to include BOTH periods
# -------------------------------------------------------

plot_dt_expanded <- rbindlist(list(
  plot_dt[Year >= 2000 & Year <= 2019][, period := "2000–2019"],
  plot_dt[Year >= 2010 & Year <= 2019][, period := "2010–2019"]
), use.names = TRUE)

plot_dt_expanded[, indicator_label := fcase(
  indicator == "diagnosed_change",  "Diagnosed",
  indicator == "treated_change",    "Treated",
  indicator == "controlled_change", "Controlled"
)]

plot_dt_expanded[, indicator_label :=
                   factor(indicator_label,
                          levels = c("Diagnosed", "Treated", "Controlled"))]

# -------------------------------------------------------
# Final boxplot
# -------------------------------------------------------

g <- ggplot(plot_dt_expanded, aes(x = indicator_label, y = change_pct, fill = period)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Sex) +
  labs(
    title = "Year-to-Year % Point Change in Hypertension Cascade Indicators",
    subtitle = "Both comparison periods represented for each year",
    x = "Indicator",
    y = "Change (percentage points)",
    fill = "Period"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )
g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_plot_past_htn_scaleup.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)

# plot the average year growth in controled implied to achieving 50% control by 2030, 2040 , 2050

# filter 2019 and asign as baseline 2025

dt_aroc <- dt_ncdrisk[Year==2019,]

dt_aroc <- dt_aroc[,list(controlled=mean(controlled)),by=list(Country,ISO)]

start_year <- 2026
target_control <- 0.5

dt_aroc[, control_2030 := pmax(0, (target_control - controlled) / (2030 - start_year))]
dt_aroc[, control_2040 := pmax(0, (target_control - controlled) / (2040 - start_year))]
dt_aroc[, control_2050 := pmax(0, (target_control - controlled) / (2050 - start_year))]


# reshape to long
plot_dt <- melt(
  dt_aroc,
  id.vars = c("Country", "ISO"),
  measure.vars = c("control_2030", "control_2040", "control_2050"),
  variable.name = "target_year",
  value.name = "aroc"
)

# clean target year labels
plot_dt[, target_year := fifelse(
  target_year == "control_2030", "2030",
  fifelse(target_year == "control_2040", "2040", "2050")
)]

library(ggdist)

g <- ggplot(plot_dt, aes(x = target_year, y = aroc, fill = target_year)) +
  # density
  stat_halfeye(
    adjust = 0.8,
    width = 0.6,
    justification = -0.25,
    alpha = 0.4,
    slab_color = NA
  ) +
  # beeswarm
  geom_beeswarm(
    aes(color = target_year),
    size = 1.8,
    alpha = 0.7,
    priority = "density"
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    x = "Target year",
    y = "Required annual increase in control (pp/year)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none"
  )

g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_beesplot_htn_scaleup.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)

# Cleaning
rm(dt,dt_ncdrisk,dt_change,dt_plot,g,plot_dt,plot_dt_expanded)
```

## Salt Reduction
```{r out-policy-salt, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# Salt reduction

dt_pop <- wpp.adj[year_id==2019,list(Nx=sum(Nx,na.rm = T)),by=list(location)]

dt_sodium_scenarios <- readRDS(file = paste0(wd_data,"Sodium/", "sodium_policy_scenarios.rds"))

dt_sodium_scenarios    <- dt_sodium_scenarios[location %in% unique(covfxn$location),]

dt_sodium_scenarios_19 <- merge(dt_sodium_scenarios,dt_pop,by=c("location"),all.x = T)

dt_sodium_scenarios_19<- dt_sodium_scenarios_19[year==2019,]

# weigted mean sodium intake
mean_sodium_intake <- weighted.mean(dt_sodium_scenarios_19$sodium_current,dt_sodium_scenarios_19$Nx)

# no sodium less that 2 as in the code
dt_sodium_scenarios[sodium_current<2,sodium_current:=2]
dt_sodium_scenarios[sodium_target_asp<2,sodium_target_asp:=2]

# Salt Map Data
dt_salt <- dt_sodium_scenarios[year==2024, c("location","sodium_current"),with = FALSE]


# Merge with countries data
dt_plot <- merge(dt_salt, countries_dt, by = "location", all.x = TRUE)

# Get world map as an sf object
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# Merge your data with the world map data (using the ISO3 codes)
world_dt <- merge(world, dt_plot, by.x = "adm0_a3", by.y = "iso3", all.x = TRUE)

# Plot the map with ggplot2
g <- ggplot(world_dt) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = sodium_current)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    oob      = squish,                # clamp values <0 or >500 back into [0,500]
    na.value = "grey90",
    name     = "g",
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title   = "Mean Sodium Intake in Grams per Day 2024",
    caption = "Source: IHME GBD 2023"
  )

g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_map_salt_female.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)


#dt_sodium_scenarios <- readRDS(file = paste0(wd_data,"Sodium/", "sodium_policy_scenarios.rds"))

dt_sodium_scenarios    <- dt_sodium_scenarios[location %in% unique(covfxn$location),]

g <- ggplot(dt_sodium_scenarios, aes(x = year, y = sodium_target_asp, group = location)) +

  # Grey spaghetti lines: all locations 
  geom_line(data = dt_sodium_scenarios, color = "grey70", size = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +

  # Axes and labels
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(2020, 2050, 5),
    limits = c(2020, 2051),  # includes 2050
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Population Sodium Reduction",
    #caption = "Source: GBD 2023 new data. Salt computed as 2.5 times sodium",
    x = "Year", y = "Mean Sodium Intake in Grams per Day"
  ) +
theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +  # optional: sets base font size
theme(
  plot.title = element_text(size = 28, face = "bold"),
  axis.title.x = element_text(size = 22, margin = margin(t = 16)),  # more top margin to push label down
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 22, margin = margin(r = 12)),
  axis.text = element_text(size = 20),
  plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 30, l = 10)  # ⬅️ key: increase bottom margin of the plot
)
# Show plot

# Show the plot
g

# Save the plot
ggsave(paste0(wd_outp, "out_policy_sodium.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 6, dpi = 300)


# Create a scatter plot of sodium intake from IHME vs salt intake from 80-80-80 paper


# 2. Define which countries to highlight
highlighted <- c("Canada", "China", "Japan", "United States")
dt_sodium_scenarios[, highlight := ifelse(location %in% highlighted, location, "Other")]

dt_sodium_scenarios <- dt_sodium_scenarios[year==2024,]
# 3. Create a summary table with one point per highlighted country
label_points <- dt_sodium_scenarios[location %in% highlighted,
                                    .(sodium_x = mean(sodium_current * 2.5),
                                      salt_y   = mean(salt_current)),
                                    by = location]

# 4. Create the plot
g <- ggplot(dt_sodium_scenarios, aes(x = sodium_current * 2.5, y = salt_current)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = highlight), alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Canada" = "red",
    "China" = "blue",
    "Japan" = "green",
    "United States" = "purple",
    "Other" = "grey70"
  )) +
  
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  
  geom_text_repel(
    data = label_points,
    aes(x = sodium_x, y = salt_y, label = location),
    size = 6,               # doubled from 3–4 to ~6
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
    # Axis labels and limits
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 20)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 20)) +
  
  labs(
    x     = "Salt intake (g/day) from GBD 2023",
    y     = "Salt intake (g/day) from GBD 2017",
    title = "",
    color = ""
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 22) +  # base font size for entire plot
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20)
  )

g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_salt_gbd17_gbd23.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 6, dpi =300)

fwrite(dt_sodium_scenarios,file=paste0(wd,"dt_sodium_scenarios.csv"))


# Comparison with Powles 2010

dt_sodium_2010 <- fread(file=paste0("C:/Users/wrgar/OneDrive - UW/02Work/ResolveToSaveLives/100MLives/data/raw/","dt_sodium_powles_2013.csv"))

dt_sodium_scenarios <- merge(dt_sodium_scenarios,dt_sodium_2010,by="location",all.x=T)

# 3. Create a summary table with one point per highlighted country
label_points <- dt_sodium_scenarios[location %in% highlighted,
                                    .(sodium_x = mean(sodium_current * 2.5),
                                      salt_y   = mean(sodium_powles_2010)*2.5),
                                    by = location]

# 4. Create the plot
g <- ggplot(dt_sodium_scenarios, aes(x = sodium_current * 2.5, y = sodium_powles_2010*2.5)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = highlight), alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Canada" = "red",
    "China" = "blue",
    "Japan" = "green",
    "United States" = "purple",
    "Other" = "grey70"
  )) +
  
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  
  geom_text_repel(
    data = label_points,
    aes(x = sodium_x, y = salt_y, label = location),
    size = 6,               # doubled from 3–4 to ~6
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
    # Axis labels and limits
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 20)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 20)) +
  
  labs(
    x     = "Salt intake (g/day) from GBD 2023",
    y     = "Salt intake (g/day) from GBD 2010",
    title = "",
    color = ""
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 22) +  # base font size for entire plot
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20)
  )

g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_salt_gbd10_gbd23.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 6, dpi =300)
```

## Trans Fat

### TFA Baseline and BPP

```{r out-policy-tf, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}

# TFA data
dt_tfa <- readRDS(file = paste0(wd_data,"TFAPolicy/", "tfa_data.rds"))

dt_tfa[location=="United States of America",location:="United States"]
dt_tfa[location=="Bolivia (Plurinational State of)",location:="Bolivia"]
dt_tfa[location=="United Republic of Tanzania",location:="Tanzania"]

dt_tfa <- dt_tfa[age=="50 to 54" & year==2024,]

dt_tfa_scenarios_19 <- merge(dt_tfa,dt_pop,by=c("location"),all.x = T)

#dt_tfa_scenarios_19<- dt_tfa_scenarios_19[year==2019,]

dt_tfa_scenarios_19 <- dt_tfa_scenarios_19[!is.na(tfa_current) & !is.na(Nx), ]

# weigted mean tfa intake
mean_tfa_intake <- weighted.mean(x=dt_tfa_scenarios_19$tfa_current,w=dt_tfa_scenarios_19$Nx)


# TFA World consumption

dt_plot <- dt_tfa

dt_plot[, indicator := tfa_current*100]

# Merge with countries data
dt_plot <- merge(dt_plot, countries_dt, by = "location", all.x = TRUE)

# Get world map as an sf object
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# Merge your data with the world map data (using the ISO3 codes)
world_dt <- merge(world, dt_plot, by.x = "adm0_a3", by.y = "iso3", all.x = TRUE)

# Plot the map with ggplot2
g <- ggplot(data = world_dt[sex="Male",]) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = indicator)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    oob      = squish,                # clamp values <0 or >500 back into [0,500]
    na.value = "grey90",
    name     = "(%) E",
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title   = "Mean population intake of trans-fatty acids (TFA) as a percentage of total energy intake (%E) 2024. Male",
    caption = "Source: GBD 2023. Age 50 to 54 for reference"
  )

g
ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_map_tfa_intake_male.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)


# Plot the map with ggplot2
g <- ggplot(data = world_dt[sex="Female",]) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = indicator)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    oob      = squish,                # clamp values <0 or >500 back into [0,500]
    na.value = "grey90",
    name     = "(%) E",
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title   = "Mean population intake of trans-fatty acids (TFA) as a percentage of total energy intake (%E) 2024. Female",
    caption = "Source: GBD 2023. Age 50 to 54 for reference"
  )

g
ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_map_tfa_intake_female.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)

# Trans Fat elimination BPP-
## Option 1: Dot plot with segments (best for showing exact timelines)
g <- ggplot(dt_tfa[!is.na(tfa_bpp_date_passed)], aes(x = tfa_bpp_date_in_effect, y = reorder(location, tfa_bpp_date_passed))) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = tfa_bpp_date_in_effect, yend = location), 
               color = "gray70", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(color = tfa_current), size = 3) +
  geom_point(aes(x = tfa_bpp_date_in_effect), shape = 17, size = 3) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red", name = "TFA Current") +
  labs(title = "TFA Policy Implementation Timeline",
       subtitle = "From passage to effect",
       x = "Year", y = "Country",
       caption = "Circle = Passage date | Triangle = Implementation date") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linetype = "dotted"))

g
ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_tfa_bpp.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)

```

### TFA Reduction scenarios

```{r out-policy-tfa-scenarios, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=11, fig.height=6}

dt_tfa_scenarios <- readRDS(file = paste0(wd_data,"TFAPolicy/", "tfa_policy_scenarios.rds"))

dt_tfa_scenarios    <- dt_tfa_scenarios[location %in% unique(covfxn$location),]

dt_statins_baseline <- dt_tfa_scenarios[year==2024,]

# Create a dummy for target line
dt_statins_baseline[, d_tfa_target := as.numeric(tfa_current<0.00)]  # 0.5% target

# Preserve only the dummy and location columns
dt_statins_baseline[, c("year", "tfa_target","tfa_current") := NULL]

dt_tfa_scenarios <- merge(dt_tfa_scenarios, dt_statins_baseline, by = "location", all.x = TRUE)

# subset from 2019
dt_tfa_scenarios <- dt_tfa_scenarios[year >= 2019]


# Spaghetti Plot: All Lines in Grey
g <- ggplot(dt_tfa_scenarios, aes(x = year, y = tfa_target * 100, group = location)) +
  # Grey spaghetti lines: all locations
  geom_line(data = dt_tfa_scenarios, color = "grey70", size = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +
  
  # Axes and labels
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(2020, 2050, 5),
    limits = c(2020, 2051),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "TFA Elimination",
    x = "Year", y = "Mean TFA Intake (%E)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 28, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 20)
  )

# Show the plot
g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_policy_tfa.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 6, dpi =300)

#ggplotly(g)

# Kable with locations with d_tfa_target==0

dt_tfa_scenarios[tfa_current>0.005 & year==2024, .(location, tfa_current)] %>%
  mutate(tfa_current = round(tfa_current * 100, 2)) %>%
  kable(
    format       = "html",
    col.names    = c("Location", "TFA Current (%)"),
    align        = c("l", "r"),
    caption      = "Locations with TFA Current > 0.5% in 2024"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 14
  ) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "6cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "4cm") %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)     # bold header row
```

## Statins uptake

```{r out-policy-statins, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# Statins uptake scenario
dt_statin_scenarios <- readRDS(file = paste0(wd_data,"Statins/", "statin_data.rds"))

# Spaghetti Plot: All Lines in Grey
g <- ggplot(dt_statin_scenarios, aes(x = year, y = statins_uptake * 100, group = location)) +
  geom_line(color = "grey70", size = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(2020, 2050, 5),
    limits = c(2020, 2051),  # Fix clipping issue if present
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_color_grey(start = 0.9, end = 0.3) +
  guides(color = FALSE) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Improved Statin Uptake",
    x = "Year", y = "Risk Population with Statins Therapy (%)"
  ) +
  
theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +  # optional: sets base font size
theme(
  plot.title = element_text(size = 28, face = "bold"),
  axis.title.x = element_text(size = 22, margin = margin(t = 16)),  # more top margin to push label down
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 22, margin = margin(r = 12)),
  axis.text = element_text(size = 20),
  plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 30, l = 10)  # ⬅️ key: increase bottom margin of the plot
)
# Show plot
g

# Save plot
ggsave(paste0(wd_outp, "out_policy_statin.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# plot the average year growth in statins coverage implied to achieving 60% control by 2030, 2040 , 2050

# filter 2019 and asign as baseline 2025
dt_aroc <- dt_statin_scenarios[year==2024,]

dt_aroc <- dt_aroc[,list(statins_current=mean(statins_current)),by=list(location)]
start_year <- 2026
target_statins <- 0.6
dt_aroc[, statins_2030 := pmax(0, (target_statins - statins_current) / (2030 - start_year))]
dt_aroc[, statins_2040 := pmax(0, (target_statins - statins_current) / (2040 - start_year))]
dt_aroc[, statins_2050 := pmax(0, (target_statins - statins_current) / (2050 - start_year))]
# reshape to long
plot_dt <- melt(
  dt_aroc,
  id.vars = c("location"),
  measure.vars = c("statins_2030", "statins_2040", "statins_2050"),
  variable.name = "target_year",
  value.name = "aroc"
)

# clean target year labels

plot_dt[, target_year := fifelse(
  target_year == "statins_2030", "2030",
  fifelse(target_year == "statins_2040", "2040", "2050")
)]

# 4. Create the plot
g <- ggplot(plot_dt, aes(x = target_year, y = aroc, fill = target_year)) +
  # density
  stat_halfeye(
    adjust = 0.8,
    width = 0.6,
    justification = -0.25,
    alpha = 0.4,
    slab_color = NA
  ) +
  # beeswarm
  geom_beeswarm(
    aes(color = target_year),
    size = 1.8,
    alpha = 0.7,
    priority = "density"
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    x     = "Target year",
    y     = "Required annual increase in statin uptake (pp/year)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none"
  )

g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_beesplot_statin_scaleup.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)

# cleaning
rm(dt_aroc,dt_statin_scenarios,plot_dt,g)
```

# Outputs

```{r output-data, message=F, warning=F, echo=F}
# Outcomes data----
#data.out <- as.data.table(readRDS(paste0(wd_outp,"searo_all_100.rds")))

files <- list.files(
  path       = paste0(wd_outp,"out_model/"), 
  pattern    = ".rds", 
  full.names = TRUE
)

dt_list <- lapply(files, function(f) {
  dt <- readRDS(f)
  setDT(dt)  # convert to data.table by reference if it isn't already
  dt
})

# Bind them all together, matching columns by name and filling missing ones
data.out <- rbindlist(dt_list, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

data.out <- data.out[!is.na(location),]

data.out[,c("eff_ir", "eff_cf"):=NULL]

# Temp filter for China
#data.out <- data.out[location %in% c("China","Iceland","Canada","United States","Germany"),]

# Define age group
#data.out[, age_group := ifelse(age < 70, "Under 70 years", "70 years or older")]

data.out[, age_group := cut(
  age,
  breaks = c(0, 70, 80, 90, Inf),
  labels = c("<70", "70-79", "80-89", "90+"),
  right  = FALSE,    # intervals are [a, b)
  include.lowest = TRUE
)]

# Add region and income group
data.out <- merge(data.out, countries_dt, by = "location", all.x = TRUE)

data.out <- data.out[!(intervention %in% c("Both","BP + Salt + TFA", "BP + Salt")),]

data.out[intervention == "BP + Salt + TFA + Statins", intervention := "All Interventions"]

data.out[intervention %in% c("BP", "Antihypertensive therapy"), intervention := "Improved Hypertension Control"]
data.out[intervention %in% c("Salt", "Salt reduction"), intervention := "Population Sodium Reduction"]
data.out[intervention == "TFA", intervention := "TFA Elimination"]
data.out[intervention == "Statins", intervention := "Improved Statin Uptake"]

data.out[intervention == "baseline", intervention := "b.a.u"]

# Exclude locations with few population and unstable estimates

locs_exclude <- NULL

# Coverage as % world population
pop_out <- wpp.adj[year_id == 2019 & age>=20, sum(Nx[location %in% unique(data.out$location)], na.rm = TRUE)]

pop_coverage <- pop_out/pop_world*100

print(paste("Population coverage of this study %: ", round(pop_coverage, 2), sep=""))

rm(wpp.adj)

dx_include <- c("All causes",
                "Ischemic heart disease",
                "Ischemic stroke",
                "Intracerebral hemorrhage",
                "Hypertensive heart disease")

cause_map <- c(
  ihd      = "Ischemic heart disease",
  istroke  = "Ischemic stroke",
  hstroke  = "Intracerebral hemorrhage",
  hhd      = "Hypertensive heart disease",
  all      = "All causes"
)

# AFTER  – define the vector once, reuse it
cause_cols <- names(cause_map)

```

## Disease Trends

```{r output-incidence, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
#baseline rates calculated in file calibration:

files <- list.files(
  path       = wd_data, 
  pattern    = "adjusted", 
  full.names = TRUE
)

dt_list <- lapply(files, function(f) {
  dt <- readRDS(f)
  setDT(dt)  # convert to data.table by reference if it isn't already
  dt
})

# Bind them all together, matching columns by name and filling missing ones
b_rates <- rbindlist(dt_list, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

b_rates <- b_rates[!is.na(location),]

# number of causes minus all
num_causes <- length(unique(cause_map))-1


# IR-Prev counts by CVD/Background

dt_mx_ob <- b_rates[year<2019,list(IR=sum(Nx*(1-PREVt0)*IR,na.rm = T),
                        PREVt0=sum(Nx*PREVt0,na.rm = T),
                        Nx=sum(Nx,na.rm =T)),
                        by=list(year)]

dt_mx <- data.out[intervention=="b.a.u" & year>= 2019,list(IR=sum(newcases,na.rm = T),
                        PREVt0=sum(sick,na.rm = T),
                        Nx=sum(pop,na.rm =T)),
                        by=list(year)]

# combine the two data.tables
dt_mx <- rbind(dt_mx_ob, dt_mx, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)


g <- ggplot(dt_mx, aes(x = year)) +
  # Specific‐cause deaths (solid pre‐2020, dashed post‐2019)
  geom_line(
    aes(
      y        = IR / 1e6,
      color    = "New-Cases",
      linetype = year > 2019
    ),
    size = 1
  ) +
  # Background deaths (also in millions), same linetype logic
  geom_line(
    aes(
      y        = PREVt0/ 1e6,
      color    = "Sick",
      linetype = year > 2019
    ),
    size = 1
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "(Million)"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = NULL,
    values = c("New-Cases" = "steelblue", "Sick" = "tomato")
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = c(`FALSE` = "solid", `TRUE` = "dashed"),
    guide  = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    x       = "Year",
    title   = "New Cases and Sick Time Series",
    caption = "Note: ages 20–95+"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.caption    = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0)
  )

g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_sick_time_total.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)


# ASMR ----

dt_mx_ob <- b_rates[year<2019,list(IR=sum(Nx*(1-PREVt0)*IR,na.rm = T),
                        PREVt0=sum(Nx*PREVt0,na.rm = T),
                        Nx=sum(Nx,na.rm =T)),
                        by=list(year,age)]

# replicate for all interventions
name_intervention <- c("Antihypertensive therapy","b.a.u","Salt reduction","Statins","TFA","All Interventions")

dt_mx_ob_ <- c()

for(int in name_intervention){
   dt_mx_ob[,intervention:=int]
   dt_mx_ob_ <- rbind(dt_mx_ob_,dt_mx_ob)  
}

dt_mx_ob <- copy(dt_mx_ob_)

rm(dt_mx_ob_)


# calculate asmr
asmr_ir_ob <- calculate_asmr(dt_mx_ob, deaths_col = "IR", population_col = "Nx",disagg_var = "intervention")
asmr_pr_ob <- calculate_asmr(dt_mx_ob, deaths_col = "PREVt0", population_col = "Nx",disagg_var = "intervention")


# 2017+
dt_mx <- data.out[intervention=="b.a.u" & year>= 2019,list(IR=sum(newcases,na.rm = T),
                        PREVt0=sum(sick,na.rm = T),
                        Nx=sum(pop,na.rm =T)),
                        by=list(year,age,intervention)]

# calculate asmr
asmr_ir <- calculate_asmr(dt_mx, deaths_col = "IR", population_col = "Nx",disagg_var = "intervention")
asmr_pr <- calculate_asmr(dt_mx, deaths_col = "PREVt0", population_col = "Nx",disagg_var = "intervention")

asmr_ir <- rbind(asmr_ir_ob, asmr_ir, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE) 

asmr_pr <- rbind(asmr_pr_ob, asmr_pr, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

# Merge the two ASMR data.tables
dt_mx <- merge(asmr_ir, asmr_pr, by = c("year", "intervention"), suffixes = c("_ir", "_pr"))
# 1. Melt to long format
dt_mx_long <- melt(
  dt_mx,
  id.vars       = c("year", "intervention"),
  measure.vars  = c("ASMR_ir", "ASMR_pr"),
  variable.name = "type",
  value.name    = "rate"
)

# 1. Compute a scaling factor so that mx_bg lines up with mx
ratio <- max(dt_mx$ASMR_ir, na.rm = TRUE) / max(dt_mx$ASMR_pr, na.rm = TRUE)

# 2. Plot with two geoms and a secondary axis

g <- ggplot(dt_mx[intervention=="b.a.u",], aes(x = year)) +
  # Specific-cause mortality (solid/dashed)
  geom_line(
    aes(
      y        = ASMR_ir,
      color    = "Incidence",
      linetype = year > 2019
    ),
    size = 1
  ) +
  # Background mortality (same units, no scaling)
  geom_line(
    aes(
      y        = ASMR_pr,
      color    = "Prevalence",
      linetype = year > 2019
    ),
    size = 1
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ intervention, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Age-Standardized Incidence Rate and Prevalence (per 100,000 population)"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = NULL,
    values = c("Incidence" = "steelblue", "Prevalence" = "tomato")
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = c(`FALSE` = "solid", `TRUE` = "dashed"),
    guide  = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    x       = "Year",
    title   = "Age-Standardized Incidence Rate and Prevalence by Cause and Intervention",
    caption = "Note: Standardized using WHO World Standard Population (2000–2025), rescaled to ages 20–95+"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.caption    = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0)
  )

g


ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_sick_time_asrates.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)

rm(dt_mx_ob, asmr_dx_ob, asmr_bg_ob, asmr_dx, asmr_bg, dt_mx_long, ratio)
```

## Mortality Trends

```{r output-mortality, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
#baseline rates calculated in file calibration:

# files <- list.files(
#   path       = wd_data, 
#   pattern    = "adjusted", 
#   full.names = TRUE
# )
# 
# dt_list <- lapply(files, function(f) {
#   dt <- readRDS(f)
#   setDT(dt)  # convert to data.table by reference if it isn't already
#   dt
# })
# 
# # Bind them all together, matching columns by name and filling missing ones
# b_rates <- rbindlist(dt_list, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

# number of causes minus all
num_causes <- length(unique(cause_map))-1

# Deaths from UNWPP total

dt_unwpp_deaths <- readRDS(file = paste0(wd_data,"UN2024/","DeathsSingleAge0050.rds"))

dt_mx_ob <- dt_unwpp_deaths[year<=2050,list(deaths_all=sum(deaths_unwpp,na.rm = T)),
                        by=list(year)]

dt_mx_ob[,source:="UNWPP 2024"]
dt_mx <- data.out[intervention=="b.a.u" & year>= 2019,list(deaths_dx=sum(dead,na.rm = T),
                        deaths_all=sum(all.mx,na.rm = T)/num_causes),by=list(year)]

dt_mx[,deaths_bg:= deaths_all - deaths_dx]

dt_mx[,source:="Model"]

dt_mx <- rbind(dt_mx_ob, dt_mx, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

dt_mx <- melt(dt_mx,id.vars = c("year","source"))

# Plotting population ratio by country, year and wrapped sex
g <- ggplot(dt_mx, aes(x = year, y = value*1, group = interaction(source, variable), color = interaction(source, variable))) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Deaths by Year and Source",
    subtitle = "UN Projections vs Model",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Deaths",
    color = "Source x Type"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  theme_minimal()

g

# Death counts by sex

dt_mx_un <- dt_unwpp_deaths[year<=2050,list(deaths_all=sum(deaths_unwpp,na.rm = T)),
                        by=list(year,sex)]

dt_mx_un[,source:="UNWPP 2024"]


dt_mx_ob <- b_rates[year<2019,list(deaths_dx=sum(Nx*DIS.mx.t0,na.rm = T),
                        deaths_bg=sum(Nx*BG.mx.all,na.rm = T)/num_causes),
                        by=list(year,sex)]

dt_mx_ob[,deaths_all:= deaths_dx + deaths_bg]

dt_mx <- data.out[intervention=="b.a.u" & year>= 2019,list(
                        deaths_all=sum(all.mx,na.rm = T)/num_causes),by=list(year,sex)]

# combine the two data.tables
dt_mx <- rbind(dt_mx_ob, dt_mx, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

dt_mx[,source:="This study"]

dt_mx <- rbind(dt_mx, dt_mx_un, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

# Create a column to indicate if year > 2019
dt_mx[, line_type := ifelse(year > 2019, "dashed", "solid")]

# Plot
g <- ggplot(dt_mx, aes(x = year, y = deaths_all/1e6, color = source, linetype = line_type)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ sex) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +  # You can try "Set1", "Paired", etc.
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = c("solid" = "solid", "dashed" = "dashed"),
    guide = "none"  # Removes linetype from legend
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(2000, 2051)) +  # Expand x-axis
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Deaths (Million)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +  # Double the base font size
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Remove linetype label just in case
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 26, face = "bold")
  )

g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_unwpp_model_deaths.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 6, dpi =300)

rm(dt_unwpp_deaths)

# Death counts by CVD/Background

dt_mx_ob <- b_rates[year<2019,list(deaths_dx=sum(Nx*DIS.mx.t0,na.rm = T),
                        deaths_bg=sum(Nx*BG.mx.all,na.rm = T)/num_causes,
                        Nx=sum(Nx,na.rm =T)),
                        by=list(year)]

dt_mx <- data.out[intervention=="b.a.u" & year>= 2019,list(deaths_dx=sum(dead,na.rm = T),
                        deaths_all=sum(all.mx,na.rm = T)/num_causes,
                        deaths_bg=sum(all.mx,na.rm = T)/num_causes-(sum(dead,na.rm = T)),
                        Nx=sum(pop,na.rm = T)),by=list(year)]

# combine the two data.tables
dt_mx <- rbind(dt_mx_ob, dt_mx, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)


g <- ggplot(dt_mx, aes(x = year)) +
  # Specific‐cause deaths (solid pre‐2020, dashed post‐2019)
  geom_line(
    aes(
      y        = deaths_dx / 1e6,
      color    = "Specific-cause",
      linetype = year > 2019
    ),
    size = 1
  ) +
  # Background deaths (also in millions), same linetype logic
  geom_line(
    aes(
      y        = deaths_bg / 1e6,
      color    = "Background",
      linetype = year > 2019
    ),
    size = 1
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Deaths (Million)"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = NULL,
    values = c("Specific-cause" = "steelblue", "Background" = "tomato")
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = c(`FALSE` = "solid", `TRUE` = "dashed"),
    guide  = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    x       = "Year",
    title   = "Deaths by Cause",
    caption = "Note: Standardized using WHO World Standard Population (2000–2025), rescaled to ages 20–95+"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.caption    = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0)
  )

g

# to compare 35.6 million deaths in 2050 from CVD
#https://www.researchgate.net/publication/384038928_Global_burden_of_cardiovascular_diseases_projections_from_2025_to_2050

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_deaths_time_total.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)


# ASMR ----

dt_mx_ob <- b_rates[year<2019,list(deaths_dx=sum(Nx*DIS.mx.t0,na.rm = T),
                        deaths_bg=sum(Nx*BG.mx.all,na.rm = T)/num_causes,
                        Nx=sum(Nx,na.rm =T)),
                        by=list(age,year)]

# replicate for all interventions
name_intervention <- c("Improved Hypertension Control","b.a.u","Population Sodium Reduction","Improved Statin Uptake","TFA Elimination","All Interventions")

dt_mx_ob_ <- c()

for(int in name_intervention){
   dt_mx_ob[,intervention:=int]
   dt_mx_ob_ <- rbind(dt_mx_ob_,dt_mx_ob)  
}

dt_mx_ob <- copy(dt_mx_ob_)

rm(dt_mx_ob_)


# calculate asmr
asmr_dx_ob <- calculate_asmr(dt_mx_ob, deaths_col = "deaths_dx", population_col = "Nx",disagg_var = "intervention")
asmr_bg_ob <- calculate_asmr(dt_mx_ob, deaths_col = "deaths_bg", population_col = "Nx",disagg_var = "intervention")


# 2017+
dt_mx <- data.out[ year>=2019,list(deaths_dx=sum(dead,na.rm = T),
                        deaths_all=sum(all.mx,na.rm = T)/num_causes,
                        deaths_bg=sum(all.mx,na.rm = T)/num_causes-sum(dead,na.rm = T),
                        pop=sum(pop,na.rm = T),
                        well=sum(well,na.rm = T),
                        sick=sum(sick,na.rm = T)),by=list(intervention,age,year)]

dt_mx[,Nx:=well+sick]

# calculate asmr
asmr_dx <- calculate_asmr(dt_mx, deaths_col = "deaths_dx", population_col = "Nx",disagg_var = "intervention")
asmr_bg <- calculate_asmr(dt_mx, deaths_col = "deaths_bg", population_col = "Nx",disagg_var = "intervention")

asmr_dx <- rbind(asmr_dx_ob, asmr_dx, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE) 

asmr_bg <- rbind(asmr_bg_ob, asmr_bg, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

# Merge the two ASMR data.tables
dt_mx <- merge(asmr_dx, asmr_bg, by = c("year", "intervention"), suffixes = c("_dx", "_bg"))
# 1. Melt to long format
dt_mx_long <- melt(
  dt_mx,
  id.vars       = c("year", "intervention"),
  measure.vars  = c("ASMR_dx", "ASMR_bg"),
  variable.name = "mortality_type",
  value.name    = "rate"
)

# 1. Compute a scaling factor so that mx_bg lines up with mx
ratio <- max(dt_mx$ASMR_dx, na.rm = TRUE) / max(dt_mx$ASMR_bg, na.rm = TRUE)

# 2. Plot with two geoms and a secondary axis

# Reorder the factor levels
dt_mx[, intervention := fcase(
  intervention %in% c("b.a.u", "BAU", "Business-as-usual"), "Business-as-Usual",
  intervention %in% c("Antihypertensive therapy", "Improved Hypertension Control"), "Improved Hypertension Control",
  intervention %in% c("Salt reduction", "Population Sodium Reduction"), "Population Sodium Reduction",
  intervention %in% c("TFA", "TFA Elimination"), "TFA Elimination",
  intervention %in% c("Improved Statin Uptake"), "Improved Statin Uptake",
  intervention %in% c("All Interventions"), "All Interventions"
)]

# Explicit ordering for facet_wrap
dt_mx[, intervention := factor(
  intervention,
  levels = c(
    "Business-as-Usual",
    "Improved Hypertension Control",
    "Population Sodium Reduction",
    "TFA Elimination",
    "Improved Statin Uptake",  # keep statins if you still want it
    "All Interventions"
  )
)]


 
g <- ggplot(dt_mx, aes(x = year)) +
  # Specific-cause mortality (solid/dashed)
  geom_line(
    aes(
      y        = ASMR_dx,
      color    = "CVD Specific-cause",
      linetype = year > 2019
    ),
    size = 2
  ) +
  # Background mortality (same units, no scaling)
  geom_line(
    aes(
      y        = ASMR_bg,
      color    = "Background",
      linetype = year > 2019
    ),
    size = 2
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ intervention, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Age-Standardized Mortality Rate (per 100,000 population)"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = NULL,
    values = c("CVD Specific-cause" = "steelblue", "Background" = "tomato")
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = c(`FALSE` = "solid", `TRUE` = "dashed"),
    guide  = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    x       = "Year",
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 28),   # facet labels
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text     = element_text(size = 20),
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 28),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 22),
    plot.caption    = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0),
    axis.title      = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.text       = element_text(size = 20)
  )

g


ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_deaths_time_cum.png"), plot = g, width = 22, height = 13, dpi =300)

rm(dt_mx_ob, asmr_dx_ob, asmr_bg_ob, asmr_dx, asmr_bg,dt_mx_long, ratio)
```



```{r output-mortality2, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
#baseline rates calculated in file calibration:

b_rates[, age_group := cut(
  age,
  breaks = c(0, 70, 80, 90, Inf),
  labels = c("<70", "70-79", "80-89", "90+"),
  right  = FALSE,    # intervals are [a, b)
  include.lowest = TRUE
)]

# Death counts by CVD/Background age group

dt_mx_ob <- b_rates[year<2019,list(deaths_dx=sum(Nx*DIS.mx.t0,na.rm = T),
                        deaths_bg=sum(Nx*BG.mx.all,na.rm = T)/num_causes,
                        Nx=sum(Nx,na.rm =T)),
                        by=list(year,age_group,sex)]

dt_mx <- data.out[intervention=="b.a.u" & year>= 2019,list(deaths_dx=sum(dead,na.rm = T),
                        deaths_all=sum(all.mx,na.rm = T)/num_causes,
                        deaths_bg=sum(all.mx,na.rm = T)/num_causes-(sum(dead,na.rm = T)),
                        Nx=sum(pop,na.rm = T)),by=list(year,age_group,sex)]

# combine the two data.tables
dt_mx <- rbind(dt_mx_ob, dt_mx, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

dt_mx[,nmx_dx:=deaths_dx/Nx]
dt_mx[,nmx_bg:=deaths_bg/Nx]

dt_mx <- dt_mx[year %in% seq(2000,2050,by=5),]

g <- ggplot(dt_mx, aes(x = factor(age_group), y = nmx_dx*1e5, fill = factor(year))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
  facet_wrap(~sex) +
  labs(
    title = "Cause-specific mortality rates",
    x = "Age Group",
    y = "Cause-specific mortality rates per 100,000  population",
    fill = "Year"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_deaths_nmx_barplot.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)

rm(dt_mx_ob, dt_mx)
```


## Comparison to IHME Foresight


```{r valida-ihme-foresight, echo=F, message=F, warning=F,fig.width=10, fig.height=6}

# Validation of deaths forecast vs IHME Foresight # ASR

# ASMR ----

dt_mx_ob <- b_rates[year<2019,list(deaths_dx=sum(Nx*DIS.mx.t0,na.rm = T),
                        Nx=sum(Nx,na.rm =T)),
                        by=list(age,year,cause)]

dt_mx_ob[, cause := fcase(
  cause == "Ischemic heart disease", "ihd",
  cause == "Ischemic stroke", "istroke",
  cause == "Intracerebral hemorrhage", "hstroke",
  cause == "Hypertensive heart disease", "hhd",
  cause == "Alzheimer's disease and other dementias", "aod",
  default = cause
)]

# calculate asmr
asmr_dx_ob <- calculate_asmr(dt_mx_ob, deaths_col = "deaths_dx", population_col = "Nx",disagg_var = "cause")

# 2017+
dt_mx <- data.out[intervention=="b.a.u" & year>=2019,list(deaths_dx=sum(dead,na.rm = T),
                        pop=sum(pop,na.rm = T),
                        well=sum(well,na.rm = T),
                        sick=sum(sick,na.rm = T)),by=list(cause,age,year)]

dt_mx[,Nx:=well+sick]

# calculate asmr
asmr_dx <- calculate_asmr(dt_mx, deaths_col = "deaths_dx", population_col = "Nx",disagg_var = "cause")

asmr_dx <- rbind(asmr_dx_ob, asmr_dx, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE) 

asmr_dx[,source:="This Study"]

# IHME


files <- list.files(path = "C:/Users/wrgar/OneDrive - UW/02Work/ResolveToSaveLives/100MLives/data/raw/GBD", 
                    pattern = "gbd2023_foresight_asr_.*20\\+\\.csv$", 
                    full.names = TRUE)

# Read and bind them
dt_gbd_fore <- rbindlist(lapply(files, fread), fill = TRUE, idcol = "source")

setnames(dt_gbd_fore, 
         tolower(gsub(" ", "_", names(dt_gbd_fore))))

dt_gbd_fore <- dt_gbd_fore[year>=2000 & scenario %in% c("Past","Reference") & !is.na(value),
                           c("year","cause_of_death_or_injury","value"),with=F]

dt_gbd_fore[,source:="GBD Foresight"]

setnames(dt_gbd_fore,c("cause_of_death_or_injury","value"),c("cause","ASMR"))

dt_gbd_fore[, cause := fcase(
  cause == "Ischemic heart disease", "ihd",
  cause == "Ischemic stroke", "istroke",
  cause == "Intracerebral hemorrhage", "hstroke",
  cause == "Hypertensive heart disease", "hhd",
  cause == "Alzheimer's disease and other dementias", "aod",
  default = cause
)]

#dt_mx$Nx <- dt_mx$deaths_dx <-NULL
dt <- rbind(asmr_dx,dt_gbd_fore,use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

dt_2019 <- dt[year==2019,]
dt_2019[,year:=NULL]

setnames(dt_2019,"ASMR","ASMRB")

dt <- merge(dt,dt_2019,all.x = T)

dt[,index:=ASMR/ASMRB*100]

dt[, cause := fcase(
  cause == "ihd","Ischemic heart disease", 
  cause == "istroke","Ischemic stroke", 
  cause == "hstroke","Intracerebral hemorrhage", 
  cause == "hhd","Hypertensive heart disease", 
  cause == "aod","Alzheimer's disease and other dementias", 
  default = cause
)]

g <- ggplot(dt, aes(x = year, y = index)) +
  # Solid lines for years <= 2019
  geom_line(
    data = dt[year <= 2019],
    aes(color = source, group = source),
    linetype = "solid",
    size = 1
  ) +
  # Dashed lines for years > 2019
  geom_line(
    data = dt[year > 2019],
    aes(color = source, group = source),
    linetype = "dashed",
    size = 1
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~cause, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "ASMR | Global | Both sexes | Ages 20+ years",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Age-Standardized Mortality Rates Index (2019=100)",
    color = "Source",
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, color = "gray30"),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray40")
  )
g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"validation_gbd_asr_foresight.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 6, dpi =300)

rm(dt,dt_gbd_fore,dt_2019,asmr_dx)
```

```{r valida-ihme-foresight2, echo=F, message=F, warning=F,fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# Validation of deaths forecast vs IHME Foresight # Deaths

files <- list.files(path = "C:/Users/wrgar/OneDrive - UW/02Work/ResolveToSaveLives/100MLives/data/raw/GBD", 
                    pattern = "gbd2023_foresight_dx_.*20\\+\\.csv$", 
                    full.names = TRUE)

# Read and bind them
dt_gbd_fore <- rbindlist(lapply(files, fread), fill = TRUE, idcol = "source")

setnames(dt_gbd_fore, 
         tolower(gsub(" ", "_", names(dt_gbd_fore))))

dt_gbd_fore <- dt_gbd_fore[year>=2000 & scenario %in% c("Past","Reference") & !is.na(value),
                           c("year","cause_of_death_or_injury","value"),with=F]

dt_gbd_fore[,source:="GBD Foresight"]

setnames(dt_gbd_fore,c("cause_of_death_or_injury","value"),c("cause","deaths_dx"))

dt_gbd_fore[, cause := fcase(
  cause == "Ischemic heart disease", "ihd",
  cause == "Ischemic stroke", "istroke",
  cause == "Intracerebral hemorrhage", "hstroke",
  cause == "Hypertensive heart disease", "hhd",
  cause == "Alzheimer's disease and other dementias", "aod",
  default = cause
)]
  
#this study
dt_mx_ob <- b_rates[year<2019,list(deaths_dx=sum(Nx*DIS.mx.t0,na.rm = T),
                        Nx=sum(Nx,na.rm =T)),
                        by=list(year,cause)]

dt_mx <- data.out[intervention=="b.a.u" & year>= 2019,list(deaths_dx=sum(dead,na.rm = T),
                        Nx=sum(pop,na.rm = T)),by=list(year,cause)]

# combine the two data.tables
dt_mx <- rbind(dt_mx_ob, dt_mx, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

dt_mx[, cause := fcase(
  cause == "Ischemic heart disease", "ihd",
  cause == "Ischemic stroke", "istroke",
  cause == "Intracerebral hemorrhage", "hstroke",
  cause == "Hypertensive heart disease", "hhd",
  cause == "Alzheimer's disease and other dementias", "aod",
  default = cause
)]

dt_mx[,death_rate:=deaths_dx/Nx*100000]

dt_mx[,source:="This Study"]

#dt_mx$Nx <- dt_mx$deaths_dx <-NULL
dt <- rbind(dt_mx,dt_gbd_fore,use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

dt[, cause := fcase(
  cause == "ihd","Ischemic heart disease", 
  cause == "istroke","Ischemic stroke", 
  cause == "hstroke","Intracerebral hemorrhage", 
  cause == "hhd","Hypertensive heart disease", 
  cause == "aod","Alzheimer's disease and other dementias", 
  default = cause
)]

g <- ggplot(dt, aes(x = year, y = deaths_dx/1e6)) +
  # Solid lines for years <= 2019
  geom_line(
    data = dt[year <= 2019],
    aes(color = source, group = source),
    linetype = "solid",
    size = 1
  ) +
  # Dashed lines for years > 2019
  geom_line(
    data = dt[year > 2019],
    aes(color = source, group = source),
    linetype = "dashed",
    size = 1
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~cause, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "Deaths| Global | Both sexes | Ages 20+ years",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Deaths (Million)",
    color = "Source",
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, color = "gray30"),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray40")
  )
g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"validation_gbd_mx_foresight.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 6, dpi =300)

rm(b_rates,dt_gbd_fore,dt_mx,dt_mx_ob)
```

## Deaths Delayed per intervention

Deaths (2020-2050) that could be delayed by time. Stacked Delayed Deaths Over Time by Intervention 2020 - 2050

```{r output-tsplot, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# Outcomes table----
dt <- data.out[year>=2025,]

# Separate baseline
baseline <- dt[intervention == "b.a.u", list(baseline_deaths = sum(dead)), 
               by = list(age_group, sex, year, location, cause, age)]

# Merge with other interventions
dt_nonbaseline <- dt[!(intervention %in% c("b.a.u","BP + Salt", "Both", "BP + Salt + TFA","BP + Salt + TFA + Statins"))]

dt_nonbaseline <- dt_nonbaseline[, list(dead = sum(dead)), 
               by = list(age_group, sex, year, location, cause, age,intervention)]

dt_merged <- merge(dt_nonbaseline, baseline, 
                   by = c("age_group", "sex", "year", "location", "cause", "age"),
                   all.x = TRUE)

# Calculate deaths delayed
dt_merged[, deaths_delayed := baseline_deaths - dead]

# Sum deaths delayed per intervention
df <- dt_merged[, .(deaths_delayed = sum(deaths_delayed, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                     by = .(year,intervention)]

df <- df[intervention %in% c("Improved Hypertension Control",
                                      "Population Sodium Reduction", 
                                      "TFA Elimination",
                                      "Improved Statin Uptake"),]

df[, intervention := factor(
  intervention,
  levels = rev(c("Improved Hypertension Control",
                 "Population Sodium Reduction", 
                 "TFA Elimination",
                 "Improved Statin Uptake"))
)]


g <- ggplot(df[!(intervention %in% c("b.a.u","Both", "BP + Salt + TFA","BP + Salt + TFA + Statins","All Interventions")),], aes(x = year, y = deaths_delayed, fill = intervention)) +
  geom_area(
    position = "stack",    # stack the values
    color    = "grey20",   # outlines for each band
    size     = 0.2,        # thin border
    alpha    = 0.8         # slight transparency
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    title    = "",
    x        = "Year",
    y        = "Deaths Delayed",
    fill     = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",           # move legend to bottom
    legend.box      = "horizontal",       # arrange items horizontally
    legend.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    legend.text     = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
g
ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_deaths_time.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 6, dpi =300)

# order by intervention, then year
setorder(df, intervention, year)

# add a cumulative deaths_delayed column
df[, cum_deaths_delayed := cumsum(deaths_delayed), by = intervention]


g <- ggplot(df[!(intervention %in% c("b.a.u","Both", "BP + Salt + TFA","BP + Salt + TFA + Statins","All Interventions")),], aes(x = year, y = cum_deaths_delayed, fill = intervention)) +
  geom_area(
    position = "stack",    # stack the values
    color    = "grey20",   # outlines for each band
    size     = 0.2,        # thin border
    alpha    = 0.8         # slight transparency
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    title    = "",
    x        = "Year",
    y        = "Deaths Delayed",
    fill     = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",           # move legend to bottom
    legend.box      = "horizontal",       # arrange items horizontally
    legend.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    legend.text     = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
g
ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_deaths_time_cum.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 6, dpi =300)

```


Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by intervention.

```{r output-table1, message=F, warning=F, echo=F}

# Outcomes table----
#dt <- data.out[year>=2025,]

# Separate baseline
baseline <- data.out[intervention == "b.a.u" & year>=2025, list(baseline_deaths = sum(dead)), 
               by = list(age_group, sex, year, location, cause, age)]

# Merge with other interventions
dt_nonbaseline <- data.out[year>=2025 & !(intervention %in% c("b.a.u"))]

dt_nonbaseline <- dt_nonbaseline[, list(dead = sum(dead)), 
               by = list(age_group, sex, year, location, cause, age,intervention)]

dt_merged <- merge(dt_nonbaseline, baseline, 
                   by = c("age_group", "sex", "year", "location", "cause", "age"),
                   all.x = TRUE)

# Calculate deaths delayed
dt_merged[, deaths_delayed := baseline_deaths - dead]

# Sum deaths delayed per intervention
dt_tab <- dt_merged[, .(deaths_delayed = sum(deaths_delayed, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                     by = .(intervention)]

# Remove "Business as usual" from the table
dt_tab <- dt_tab[intervention != "b.a.u"]

# knitr::kable(dt_tab, caption = "Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by intervention",
#              digits = 1)

dt_tab %>%
  mutate(deaths_delayed = comma(deaths_delayed)) %>%
  kable(
    format       = "html",
    col.names    = c("Intervention", "Deaths Delayed"),
    align        = c("l", "r"),
    caption      = "Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by intervention"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 14
  ) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "6cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "4cm") %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)     # bold header row
```


Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by intervention.

```{r out-tab-int-caut, message=F, warning=F, echo=F}
# Outcomes table by Intervention----
#dt <- data.out[year>=2025,]

## 1. baseline totals --------------------------------------------------
baseline <- data.out[intervention == "b.a.u" & year>=2025,
               .(baseline_deaths = sum(dead, na.rm = TRUE)),
               by = "intervention"]

baseline[,c("intervention"):=NULL]

## 2. non-baseline observations --------------------------------------
nonbaseline <- data.out[intervention != "b.a.u" & year>=2025,]

nonbaseline <- nonbaseline[,
               .(deaths_projected = sum(dead, na.rm = TRUE)),
               by = "intervention"]
  
## 3. attach the baseline deaths -------------------------------------

merged <- cbind(nonbaseline, baseline)

## 4. deaths avoided / delayed ---------------------------------------
merged[, deaths_delayed := (baseline_deaths - deaths_projected)]

## 5. final summary — *same* by_cols ----------------------------------
merged[, deaths_delayed_ave := deaths_delayed/25]

merged[, deaths_delayed_pct := deaths_delayed/baseline_deaths]

  
merged %>%
  mutate(deaths_projected = comma(deaths_projected,accuracy = 1),
         baseline_deaths   = comma(baseline_deaths,accuracy = 1),
         deaths_delayed   = comma(deaths_delayed,accuracy = 1),
         deaths_delayed_ave   = comma(deaths_delayed_ave,accuracy = 1),
         deaths_delayed_pct = round(deaths_delayed_pct*100,2)) %>%
  kable(
    format       = "html",
    col.names    = c("Intervention", "Projected Deaths", "Baseline Deaths", "Deaths Delayed", "Deaths Delayed per Year",  "Deaths Delayed (%)"),
    align        = c("l","r", "r", "r", "r", "r"),
    caption      = "Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by Country"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 14
  ) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "6cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "4cm") %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)     # bold header row

# Save for exporting to excel
dt_out_total <- copy(merged)
```

```{r out-tab-int-cau, message=F, warning=F, echo=F}
# Outcomes table by Intervention*Cause----
#dt <- data.out[year>=2025,]

dt_tab <- deaths_summary(data.out[year>=2025,], 
                       baseline_label = "b.a.u",
                       by_cols        = c("intervention","cause"))

##??patch: not showing causes not affected by interventions
dt_tab <- dt_tab[!(cause %in% c("istroke", "hstroke", "hhd", "aod") & intervention == "TFA Elimination"), ]


dt_tab <- dt_tab[!(cause %in% c("hstroke", "hhd", "aod") & intervention == "Improved Statin Uptake"),]

dt_tab[,cause:=ifelse(cause=="ihd","Ischemic heart disease",
                            ifelse(cause=="istroke", "Ischemic stroke",
                                   ifelse(cause== "hstroke", "Intracerebral hemorrhage",
                                          ifelse(cause== "hhd", "Hypertensive heart disease",
                                                 ifelse(cause== "aod", "Alzheimer's disease and other dementias",
                                                        cause)))))]

dt_tab %>%
  mutate(deaths_projected = comma(deaths_projected,accuracy = 1),
         baseline_deaths   = comma(baseline_deaths,accuracy = 1),
         deaths_delayed   = comma(deaths_delayed,accuracy = 1),
         deaths_delayed_ave   = comma(deaths_delayed_ave,accuracy = 1),
         deaths_delayed_pct = round(deaths_delayed_pct*100,2)) %>%
  kable(
    format       = "html",
    col.names    = c("Intervention","Cause", "Projected Deaths", "Baseline Deaths", "Deaths Delayed", "Deaths Delayed per Year",  "Deaths Delayed (%)"),
    align        = c("l", "r","r", "r", "r", "r", "r"),
    caption      = "Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by Country"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 14
  ) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "6cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "4cm") %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)     # bold header row

# Save for exporting to excel
dt_out_1 <- copy(dt_tab)
```

## Outputs by Demographics

Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by sex and age group: under and over 70 years of age.

```{r 1out-int-age, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# deaths delayed per age group----
dt <- data.out[year>=2025,]

# Separate baseline
baseline <- dt[intervention == "b.a.u", .(baseline_deaths = dead), 
               by = .(age_group, sex, year, location, cause, age)]

# Merge with other interventions
dt_nonbaseline <- dt[intervention != "b.a.u"]

dt_merged <- merge(dt_nonbaseline, baseline, 
                   by = c("age_group", "sex", "year", "location", "cause", "age"),
                   all.x = TRUE)

# Calculate deaths delayed
dt_merged[, deaths_delayed := baseline_deaths - dead]

# Sum deaths delayed per intervention
dt_plot <- dt_merged[, .(deaths_delayed = sum(deaths_delayed, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                     by = .(intervention, age_group, sex)]
# Remove "Business as usual" from the plot
dt_plot <- dt_plot[intervention != "b.a.u"]
# Get total delayed deaths by intervention
order_levels <- dt_plot[, .(total_delayed = sum(deaths_delayed)), by = intervention][
  order(total_delayed), intervention]

# Reorder the intervention factor
dt_plot[, intervention := factor(intervention, levels = order_levels)]

# g <- ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = deaths_delayed/1000000, y = intervention, fill = age_group)) +
#   geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
#   facet_wrap(~sex, nrow = 1) +
#   scale_fill_manual(
#     values = c("Under 70 years" = "#F39C12", "70 years or older" = "#145A32"),
#     name = "Age group"
#   ) +
#   scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
#   theme(
#     panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1),
#     strip.text = element_text(size = 16),
#     axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
#     axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
#     legend.position = "bottom",
#     plot.margin = unit(c(1, 1.5, 1, 1), "cm")  # top, right, bottom, left
#   ) +
#   labs(
#     x = "Millions of deaths that could be delayed",
#     y = NULL
#   )

g <- ggplot(dt_plot,
            aes(x = deaths_delayed/1e6,
                y = intervention,
                fill = age_group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~sex, nrow = 1) +
  
  # (no scale_fill_manual — will use the default ggplot2 palette)
  
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.border    = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1),
    strip.text      = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.key.width = unit(1.2, "cm"),
    plot.margin     = unit(c(1, 1.5, 1, 1), "cm")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Millions of deaths that could be delayed",
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Age group"
  )

g


ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_barplot_deaths_delayed_age.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)


# Deaths by age
dt_tab_t <- deaths_summary(data.out[year>=2025,], 
                       baseline_label = "b.a.u",
                       by_cols        = c("age_group"))

dt_tab_a <- deaths_summary(data.out[year>=2025,], 
                       baseline_label = "b.a.u",
                       by_cols        = c("age_group", "intervention"))

dt_tab_t <- rbind(dt_tab_t, 
                  dt_tab_a, 
                  fill = TRUE, use.names = TRUE)
  
# format numbers
df_fmt <- dt_tab_t  %>%
  mutate(
    `Projected Deaths`        = comma(deaths_projected, accuracy = 1),
    `Baseline Deaths`         = comma(baseline_deaths,     accuracy = 1),
    `Deaths Delayed`          = comma(deaths_delayed,     accuracy = 1),
    `Deaths Delayed per Year` = comma(deaths_delayed_ave, accuracy = 1),
    `% Delayed`               = percent(deaths_delayed_pct, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) %>%
  select(
    factor = age_group,
    intervention = intervention,
    `Projected Deaths`,
    `Baseline Deaths` ,
    `Deaths Delayed`,
    `Deaths Delayed per Year`,
    `% Delayed`
  )

# Save for exporting to excel
dt_out_age <- copy(df_fmt)

dt_out_age$disaggregation <- "Age"

# Deaths by sex
dt_tab_t <- deaths_summary(data.out[year>=2025,], 
                       baseline_label = "b.a.u",
                       by_cols        = c("sex"))

dt_tab_a <- deaths_summary(data.out[year>=2025,], 
                       baseline_label = "b.a.u",
                       by_cols        = c("sex", "intervention"))

dt_tab_t <- rbind(dt_tab_t, 
                  dt_tab_a, 
                  fill = TRUE, use.names = TRUE)
  
# format numbers
df_fmt <- dt_tab_t  %>%
  mutate(
    `Projected Deaths`        = comma(deaths_projected, accuracy = 1),
    `Baseline Deaths`         = comma(baseline_deaths,     accuracy = 1),
    `Deaths Delayed`          = comma(deaths_delayed,     accuracy = 1),
    `Deaths Delayed per Year` = comma(deaths_delayed_ave, accuracy = 1),
    `% Delayed`               = percent(deaths_delayed_pct, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) %>%
  select(
    factor = sex,
    intervention = intervention,
    `Projected Deaths`,
    `Baseline Deaths` ,
    `Deaths Delayed`,
    `Deaths Delayed per Year`,
    `% Delayed`
  )

# Save for exporting to excel
dt_out_sex <- copy(df_fmt)

dt_out_sex$disaggregation <- "Sex"

```

Cummulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by sex and age group: cause of death.

```{r 1out-int-cause, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# deaths delayed per cause----
dt <- data.out[year>=2025,]

# Separate baseline
baseline <- dt[intervention == "b.a.u", .(baseline_deaths = dead), 
               by = .(age_group, sex, year, location, cause, age)]

# Merge with other interventions
dt_nonbaseline <- dt[intervention != "b.a.u"]

dt_merged <- merge(dt_nonbaseline, baseline, 
                   by = c("age_group", "sex", "year", "location", "cause", "age"),
                   all.x = TRUE)

# Calculate deaths delayed
dt_merged[, deaths_delayed := baseline_deaths - dead]

# Sum deaths delayed per intervention
dt_plot <- dt_merged[, .(deaths_delayed = sum(deaths_delayed, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                     by = .(intervention, cause, sex)]
# Remove "Business as usual" from the plot
dt_plot <- dt_plot[intervention != "b.a.u"]
# Get total delayed deaths by intervention
order_levels <- dt_plot[, .(total_delayed = sum(deaths_delayed)), by = intervention][
  order(total_delayed), intervention]

# Reorder the intervention factor
dt_plot[, intervention := factor(intervention, levels = order_levels)]

# Save for exporting to excel
dt_out_3 <- copy(dt_plot)

library(grid)  # for unit()

g <- ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = deaths_delayed / 1e6, y = intervention, fill = cause)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~sex, nrow = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    
    # FIXED: expand right margin to fit full legend text
    plot.margin = unit(c(1, 2.5, 1.2, 1), "cm"),  # top, right, bottom, left

    # FIXED: improve legend layout and sizing
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.8, "cm")
  ) +
  # FIXED: guide with 2 rows to avoid long horizontal stretch
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Millions of deaths that could be delayed",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  
  # COLOR and LABEL MAPPING
  scale_fill_manual(
    name   = "",
    values = c(
      aod     = "#D55E00",
      hhd     = "#E69F00",
      hstroke = "#F0E442",
      ihd     = "#56B4E9",
      istroke = "#CC79A7"
    ),
    labels = c(
      aod     = "Alzheimer's disease and other dementias",
      hhd     = "Hypertensive heart disease",
      hstroke = "Hemorrhagic stroke",
      ihd     = "Ischemic heart disease",
      istroke = "Ischemic stroke"
    )
  )

g

# Save with wider width to accommodate legend
ggsave(paste0(wd_outp, "out_barplot_deaths_delayed_cause.png"),
       plot = g, width = 11, height = 5.5, dpi = 300)
```


## Outputs by Region

Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by Country.

```{r out-tab-loc, message=F, warning=F, echo=F}
# Outcomes table by Region*Country----
#dt <- data.out[year>=2025,]

dt_tab <- deaths_summary(data.out[year>=2025,], 
                       baseline_label = "b.a.u",
                       by_cols        = c("region","location"))

dt_tab_b <- data.out[year>=2025 & intervention=="b.a.u",list(baseline_deaths=sum(dead)),by=list(region, location)]

dt_tab_b$intervention <- NULL

dt_tab <- data.out[year>=2025 & intervention=="All Interventions",list(deaths_projected=sum(dead)),by=list(region, location)]

dt_tab <- merge(dt_tab, dt_tab_b, by = c("region", "location"), all.x = TRUE)

dt_tab[, deaths_delayed := (baseline_deaths - deaths_projected)]

dt_tab[, deaths_delayed_ave := deaths_delayed/25]

dt_tab[, deaths_delayed_pct := deaths_delayed/baseline_deaths]

# format numbers
df_fmt <- dt_tab %>%
  mutate(
    `Projected Deaths`        = comma(deaths_projected, accuracy = 1),
    `Baseline Deaths`         = comma(baseline_deaths,     accuracy = 1),
    `Deaths Delayed`          = comma(deaths_delayed,     accuracy = 1),
    `Deaths Delayed per Year` = comma(deaths_delayed_ave, accuracy = 1),
    `% Delayed`               = percent(deaths_delayed_pct, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) %>%
  select(
    region,
    location,
    `Projected Deaths`,
    `Baseline Deaths` ,
    `Deaths Delayed`,
    `Deaths Delayed per Year`,
    `% Delayed`
  )

# Save for exporting to excel
dt_out_2 <- copy(df_fmt)
```
