```{r out-tab-loc, message=F, warning=F, echo=F}
# create the interactive DT widget
datatable(
  df_fmt,
  filter    = 'top',    # input boxes at top of each column
  rownames  = FALSE,
  options   = list(
    pageLength = 10,    # show 10 rows per page
    lengthMenu = c(5, 10, 25, 50),
    autoWidth  = TRUE
  )
)

# Top 10 by number of deaths delayed ----
top10_absolute <- dt_tab %>%
  arrange(desc(deaths_delayed)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  mutate(
    `Deaths Delayed` = comma(deaths_delayed, accuracy = 1)
  ) %>%
  select(region, location, `Deaths Delayed`)

datatable(
  top10_absolute,
  rownames = FALSE,
  options = list(
    dom = 't',
    pageLength = 10
  ),
  caption = "Top 10 Locations by Number of Deaths Delayed"
)

# Top 10 by percent of deaths delayed ----
top10_relative <- dt_tab %>%
  arrange(desc(deaths_delayed_pct)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  mutate(
    `% Deaths Delayed` = percent(deaths_delayed_pct, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) %>%
  select(region, location, `% Deaths Delayed`)

datatable(
  top10_relative,
  rownames = FALSE,
  options = list(
    dom = 't',
    pageLength = 10
  ),
  caption = "Top 10 Locations by % Deaths Delayed"
)


# Deaths by region
dt_tab_t <- deaths_summary(data.out[year>=2025,], 
                       baseline_label = "b.a.u",
                       by_cols        = c("region"))

dt_tab_a <- deaths_summary(data.out[year>=2025,], 
                       baseline_label = "b.a.u",
                       by_cols        = c("region", "intervention"))

dt_tab_t <- rbind(dt_tab_t, 
                  dt_tab_a, 
                  fill = TRUE, use.names = TRUE)
  
# format numbers
df_fmt <- dt_tab_t  %>%
  mutate(
    `Projected Deaths`        = comma(deaths_projected, accuracy = 1),
    `Baseline Deaths`         = comma(baseline_deaths,     accuracy = 1),
    `Deaths Delayed`          = comma(deaths_delayed,     accuracy = 1),
    `Deaths Delayed per Year` = comma(deaths_delayed_ave, accuracy = 1),
    `% Delayed`               = percent(deaths_delayed_pct, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) %>%
  select(
    factor = region,
    intervention = intervention,
    `Projected Deaths`,
    `Baseline Deaths` ,
    `Deaths Delayed`,
    `Deaths Delayed per Year`,
    `% Delayed`
  )

# Save for exporting to excel
dt_out_region <- copy(df_fmt)

dt_out_region$disaggregation <- "Region"

# Deaths by region
dt_tab_t <- deaths_summary(data.out[year>=2025,], 
                       baseline_label = "b.a.u",
                       by_cols        = c("location","cause","intervention","year"))


# format numbers
df_fmt <- dt_tab_t  %>%
  mutate(
    `Projected Deaths`        = comma(deaths_projected, accuracy = 1),
    `Baseline Deaths`         = comma(baseline_deaths,     accuracy = 1),
    `Deaths Delayed`          = comma(deaths_delayed,     accuracy = 1),
    `Deaths Delayed per Year` = comma(deaths_delayed_ave, accuracy = 1),
    `% Delayed`               = percent(deaths_delayed_pct, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) %>%
  select(
    location = location,
    cause = cause,
    intervention = intervention,
    year = year,
    `Projected Deaths`,
    `Baseline Deaths` ,
    `Deaths Delayed`,
    `Deaths Delayed per Year`,
    `% Delayed`
  )

# Save for exporting to excel
dt_out_country_year <- copy(df_fmt)

```

Cummulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by sex and age group: region.

```{r out-int-region, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# deaths delayed per intervention----
dt <- data.out[year>=2025,]

# Separate baseline
baseline <- dt[intervention == "b.a.u", .(baseline_deaths = dead), 
               by = .(age_group, sex, year, location, cause, age,region)]

# Merge with other interventions
dt_nonbaseline <- dt[intervention != "b.a.u"]

dt_merged <- merge(dt_nonbaseline, baseline, 
                   by = c("age_group", "sex", "year", "location", "cause", "age","region"),
                   all.x = TRUE)

# Calculate deaths delayed
dt_merged[, deaths_delayed := baseline_deaths - dead]

# Sum deaths delayed per intervention
dt_plot <- dt_merged[, .(deaths_delayed = sum(deaths_delayed, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                     by = .(intervention, region, sex)]
# Remove "Business as usual" from the plot
dt_plot <- dt_plot[intervention != "b.a.u"]
# Get total delayed deaths by intervention
order_levels <- dt_plot[, .(total_delayed = sum(deaths_delayed)), by = intervention][
  order(total_delayed), intervention]

# Reorder the intervention factor
dt_plot[, intervention := factor(intervention, levels = order_levels)]

# Save for exporting to excel
dt_out_4 <- copy(dt_plot)

g <- ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = deaths_delayed/1000000, y = intervention, fill = region)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~sex, nrow = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.margin = unit(c(1, 1.5, 1, 1), "cm")  # top, right, bottom, left
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Millions of deaths that could be delayed",
    y = NULL
  )
g
ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_barplot_deaths_delayed_region.png"), plot = g, width = 11, height = 5.5, dpi = 300)
```

Age-standardized avoidable death rates by country (by 100,000 population)

```{r out-map-mx, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# deaths delayed per intervention----
dt <- data.out[year>=2025,]

# Separate baseline
baseline <- dt[intervention == "b.a.u", .(baseline_deaths = dead), 
               by = .(age_group, sex, year, location, cause, age)]

# Merge with other interventions
dt_nonbaseline <- dt[intervention == "All Interventions"]

dt_merged <- merge(dt_nonbaseline, baseline, 
                   by = c("age_group", "sex", "year", "location", "cause", "age"),
                   all.x = TRUE)

# Calculate deaths delayed
dt_merged[, deaths_delayed := baseline_deaths - dead]

# Sum deaths delayed per intervention

# repeated population by intervention
n_interventions <- length(unique(dt_merged[, intervention]))
n_causes <- length(unique(dt_merged[, cause]))

dt_plot <- dt_merged[!is.na(deaths_delayed) & !is.na(pop), .(deaths = sum(baseline_deaths, na.rm = TRUE),deaths_delayed = sum(deaths_delayed, na.rm = TRUE),
                         population = sum(pop, na.rm = TRUE)/(n_interventions*n_causes)), 
                     by = .(location,sex)]

# Compute age standarized mortality rate
dt_plot[, indicator := deaths_delayed/deaths*100]

# Merge with countries data
dt_plot <- merge(dt_plot, countries_dt, by = "location", all.x = TRUE)

# Get world map as an sf object
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# Merge your data with the world map data (using the ISO3 codes)
world_dt <- merge(world, dt_plot, by.x = "adm0_a3", by.y = "iso3", all.x = TRUE)

world_dt_f <- world_dt[world_dt$sex=="Female",]

world_dt_f <- world_dt_f %>%
  mutate(indicator_bin = cut(
    indicator,
    breaks = c(-Inf, 9, 14, 19, 24, Inf),
    labels = c("5–9%",   "10–14%",  "15–19%",  "20–24%",  "25%+")
  ))

g <- ggplot(world_dt_f) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = indicator_bin), colour = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
  "5–9%"    = "#1a9850",  # green
  "10–14%"  = "#d9ef8b",  # light green
  "15–19%"  = "#fee08b",  # yellow
  "20–24%"  = "#fc8d59",  # orange
  "25%+"    = "#d73027"   # red
),
    na.value = "grey90",
    name     = "% Reduction (Female)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "",
    caption = ""
  )

g


ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_map_death_rate_female.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)

world_dt_m <- world_dt[world_dt$sex=="Male",]

# Plot the map with ggplot2
world_dt_m <- world_dt_m %>%
  mutate(indicator_bin = cut(
    indicator,
    breaks = c(-Inf, 9, 14, 19, 24, Inf),
    labels = c("5–9%",   "10–14%",  "15–19%",  "20–24%",  "25%+")
  ))

g <- ggplot(world_dt_m) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = indicator_bin), colour = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
  "5–9%"    = "#1a9850",  # green
  "10–14%"  = "#d9ef8b",  # light green
  "15–19%"  = "#fee08b",  # yellow
  "20–24%"  = "#fc8d59",  # orange
  "25%+"    = "#d73027"   # red
 ),
    na.value = "grey90",
    name     = "% Reduction (Male)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "",
    caption = ""
  )

g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_map_death_rate_male.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)

# Plot age standarized rate

# Plot the map with ggplot2
# g <- ggplot(data = world_dt_f) +
#   geom_sf(aes(fill = indicator * 100000)) +
#   scale_fill_viridis_c(
#     limits   = c(0, 500),             # set your desired range
#     oob      = squish,                # clamp values <0 or >500 back into [0,500]
#     na.value = "grey90",
#     name     = "by 100,000 population",
#     breaks   = seq(0, 500, by = 100)  # optional: control legend tick marks
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   labs(
#     title   = "Age-standardized avoidable death rates by country (Female)",
#     caption = ""
#   )
# 
# g
# 
```


# Supplemental Material


```{r output-kontis, message=F, warning=F, echo=F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
#Output comparison with Kontis 2019

dt_kontis <- read_excel(
  path       = paste0(wd_raw,"Transversals/kontis-et-al-2019- supplemental.xlsx"),
  col_names  = TRUE
)

dt_kontis <- as.data.table(dt_kontis)

# rename locations

name_map <- c(
  "United Republic of Tanzania" = "Tanzania",
  "United States of America" = "United States",
  "China, Taiwan Province of China" = "Taiwan (Province of China)",
  "Dem. People's Republic of Korea" = "Democratic People's Republic of Korea",
  "Bolivia (Plurinational State of)" = "Bolivia",
  "Micronesia (Fed. States of)"     = "Micronesia (Federated States of)", 
  "State of Palestine" =   "Palestine",
  "Czech Republic"     = "Czechia",
  "The former Yugoslav Republic of Macedonia" = "North Macedonia"
)
dt_kontis[, location := fcoalesce(name_map[location], location)]

# extract the number before the "(" and turn it into an integer
dt_kontis[, deaths_delayed := as.integer(sub("\\s*\\(.*", "", deaths_delayed))]

# merge with dt_out
dt_plot <- merge(dt_out_2,dt_kontis,by=c("location"),all.x=T,all.y=T)

dt_plot <- dt_plot[,c("location","deaths_delayed","Deaths Delayed"),with = FALSE]

# Compute age standarized mortality rate
setnames(dt_plot, "Deaths Delayed", "deaths_total")

dt_plot[, deaths_total := as.numeric(gsub(",", "", deaths_total))/1000]
dt_plot[, indicator := (deaths_delayed/deaths_total-1) * 100]

g <- ggplot(dt_plot[deaths_delayed>0 & deaths_total>0,], aes(x = "", y = indicator)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Model Output vs Kontis et al 2019 Deaths Delayed by Country",
    x     = NULL,        # no x‐axis label
    y     = "Kontis/Model-1(%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
g

# Merge with countries data

dt_plot <- dt_plot[indicator>-100,]

dt_plot <- merge(dt_plot, countries_dt, by = "location", all.x = TRUE)

# Get world map as an sf object
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# Merge your data with the world map data (using the ISO3 codes)
world_dt <- merge(world, dt_plot, by.x = "adm0_a3", by.y = "iso3", all.x = TRUE)


#Plot the map with ggplot2
g <- ggplot(data = world_dt) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = indicator)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    oob      = squish,                # clamp values <0 or >500 back into [0,500]
    na.value = "grey90",
    name     = "Kontis/Model-1(%)",
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title   = "Model Output vs Kontis et al 2019 Deaths Delayed by Country",
    caption = ""
  )

g

ggsave(paste0(wd_outp,"out_map_kontis_comparison.png"), plot = g, width = 9, height = 5, dpi =300)



```


```{r output-countrylist, message=F, warning=F, echo=F}
# Sheet 4: List of countries in each region

dt <- unique(data.out[, .(region, location)])

# 1) aggregate into a single string per region
countries_by_region <- dt[,
  .(Countries = paste0(sort(location), collapse = ", ")),
  by = .(Region = region)
]

# 2) order by Region
setorder(countries_by_region, Region)

knitr::kable(
  countries_by_region,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  caption  = "List of countries by World Bank super-region"
)

```



```{r output-excel, message=F, warning=F, echo=F}
# Create a new workbook
wb <- createWorkbook()

# Add sheets and data
dt_out_1 <- rbind(dt_out_total,dt_out_1,fill = TRUE, use.names = TRUE)

dt_out_1[is.na(cause), cause := "All CVDs" ]

setcolorder(dt_out_1, c("intervention", "cause")) 

# Sheet 1: Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by intervention and cause
addWorksheet(wb, "Cause")
writeData(wb, "Cause", dt_out_1)

# Sheet 2: Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by intervention, sex and cause

addWorksheet(wb, "Sex & Cause")
writeData(wb, "Sex & Cause", dt_out_3)

# Sheet 3: Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by intervention, region and cause
addWorksheet(wb, "Region & Cause")
writeData(wb, "Region & Cause", dt_out_4)

# Sheet 4: List of countries in each region
addWorksheet(wb, "Countries List")
writeData(wb, "Countries List", countries_by_region)

# Sheet 6: Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by country
addWorksheet(wb, "Country")
writeData(wb, "Country", dt_out_2)

# Sheet 7: Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by intervention and diaggregation
out_list <- list(dt_out_sex, dt_out_age, dt_out_region)
  
dt_out_5 <- as.data.table(rbindlist(out_list, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE))

dt_out_5 <- dt_out_5[!is.na(intervention)]

addWorksheet(wb, "Feature & Intervention")
writeData(wb, "Feature & Intervention", dt_out_5)


dt <- copy(dt_out_5)

num_cols <- c("Projected Deaths","Baseline Deaths","Deaths Delayed","Deaths Delayed per Year")
dt[, (num_cols) := lapply(.SD, function(x) as.numeric(gsub(",", "", x))), .SDcols = num_cols]
dt[, `% Delayed` := as.numeric(gsub("%","", `% Delayed`))/100]


library(forcats)
library(stringr)

# Create nested factor: "disaggregation: factor"
dt[, factor_nested := paste(disaggregation, factor, sep = " • ")]

# Order within disaggregation so it looks nested
dt[, factor_nested := forcats::fct_inorder(factor_nested)]

# Add spacing markers between disaggregation groups
unique_disag <- unique(dt$disaggregation)

factor_levels <- levels(dt$factor_nested)
new_levels <- c()
for(i in seq_along(unique_disag)) {
  group_factors <- factor_levels[grepl(paste0("^", unique_disag[i]), factor_levels)]
  new_levels <- c(new_levels, group_factors)
  if(i < length(unique_disag)) new_levels <- c(new_levels, paste0("SPACER_", i))
}

dt[, factor_nested := factor(factor_nested, levels = new_levels)]

g <- ggplot(
  dt[intervention != "All Interventions"],
  aes(x = `Deaths Delayed`, y = factor_nested, color = intervention)
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 4) +   # <- constant size for all bubbles
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_y_discrete(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15)),
    labels = function(x) ifelse(grepl("^SPACER_", x), "", x)
  ) +
  scale_color_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 22)) +
  labs(x = "Deaths Delayed", y = "", color = "") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.key.width = unit(18, "pt"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.margin = margin(20, 30, 80, 20),
    axis.text.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 5)),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3)
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off")

g

ggsave(
  paste0(wd_outp, "out_deaths_disaggregation_bubble.png"),
  plot = g, width = 11, height = 7.5, dpi = 300, limitsize = FALSE
)


# Sheewt 8: Cumulative Deaths (2025-2050) that could be delayed by country and year
# addWorksheet(wb, "Country & Year")
# writeData(wb, "Country & Year", dt_out_country_year)

addWorksheet(wb, "Kontis")
writeData(wb, "Kontis", dt_kontis)


# Sheet 9 Country_cause
# Separate baseline
baseline <- data.out[intervention == "b.a.u" & year>=2025, .(baseline_deaths = dead), 
               by = .(year,location, cause,age,sex)]

# Merge with other interventions
dt_nonbaseline <- data.out[intervention != "b.a.u" & year>=2025]

dt_merged <- merge(dt_nonbaseline, baseline, 
                   by = c("year","location", "cause","age","sex"),
                   all.x = TRUE)

# Calculate deaths delayed
dt_merged[, deaths_delayed := baseline_deaths - dead]

# Sum deaths delayed per intervention
dt_tab <- dt_merged[, .(baseline_deaths = sum(baseline_deaths, na.rm = TRUE),
                     deaths_delayed = sum(deaths_delayed, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                     by = .(location,intervention)]

dt_tab[,percent_delayed:=deaths_delayed/baseline_deaths]

addWorksheet(wb, "Location And Cause")
writeData(wb, "Location And Cause", dt_tab)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# ADD: <70 outputs to UWRTSL-100MLives_Output.xlsx
# ------------------------------------------------------------
setDT(data.out)

dt <- data.out[year >= 2025 & year <= 2050]

tab_u70_int_sex <- dt[age_group == "<70",
  .(deaths_u70 = sum(dead, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = .(intervention, sex)
][order(intervention, sex)]

# optional: pretty display
tab_u70_int_sex_print <- copy(tab_u70_int_sex)
tab_u70_int_sex_print[, deaths_u70 := scales::comma(deaths_u70, accuracy = 1)]

knitr::kable(
  tab_u70_int_sex_print,
  caption = "Cumulative deaths <70 years (2025–2050) by intervention and sex"
)

dt <- data.out[year >= 2025 & year <= 2050]

dt <- data.out[year >= 2025 & year <= 2050]

# (A) Pick top 30 most populous locations (use BAU, year 2025)
# NOTE: pop is repeated across causes, so de-duplicate by taking a single cause.
# If you have a preferred cause to use for pop, set pop_cause to that.
pop_cause <- dt[!is.na(cause), unique(cause)][1]

pop_2025 <- dt[intervention == "b.a.u" & year == 2025 & cause == pop_cause,
  .(population = sum(pop, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = .(location)
][order(-population)]

top30_locs <- pop_2025[1:30, location]

# (B) Build baseline vs all-interventions deaths by location × age_group
base_loc_age <- dt[intervention == "b.a.u",
  .(deaths_base = sum(dead, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = .(location, age_group)
]

all_loc_age <- dt[intervention == "All Interventions",
  .(deaths_allint = sum(dead, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = .(location, age_group)
]

loc_age <- merge(all_loc_age, base_loc_age,
  by = c("location", "age_group"),
  all.x = TRUE
)

# deaths delayed (baseline - all interventions)
loc_age[, deaths_delayed := deaths_base - deaths_allint]

# (C) Collapse to country-level totals:
#  - col1: <70 deaths delayed (both sexes combined)
#  - col2: share of ALL delayed deaths that are <70
tab_top30_u70_delayed <- loc_age[location %in% top30_locs,
  .(
    deaths_delayed_u70   = sum(deaths_delayed[age_group == "<70"], na.rm = TRUE),
    deaths_delayed_total = sum(deaths_delayed, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = .(location)
]

tab_top30_u70_delayed[, prop_delayed_u70 :=
  fifelse(deaths_delayed_total == 0, NA_real_, deaths_delayed_u70 / deaths_delayed_total)
]

# attach population for ordering / reporting
tab_top30_u70_delayed <- merge(
  tab_top30_u70_delayed,
  pop_2025[, .(location, population)],
  by = "location",
  all.x = TRUE
)[order(-population)]

# final table with requested columns
tab_top30_u70_delayed_out <- tab_top30_u70_delayed[, .(
  location,
  deaths_delayed_u70,
  prop_delayed_u70
)]

# optional: pretty display
tab_top30_u70_delayed_print <- copy(tab_top30_u70_delayed_out)
tab_top30_u70_delayed_print[, deaths_delayed_u70 := scales::comma(deaths_delayed_u70, accuracy = 1)]
tab_top30_u70_delayed_print[, prop_delayed_u70 := scales::percent(prop_delayed_u70, accuracy = 0.1)]

knitr::kable(
  tab_top30_u70_delayed_print,
  caption = "Top 30 most populous countries: deaths delayed <70 (All Interventions vs BAU, 2025–2050) and share of delayed deaths that are <70"
)
# 1) Small table: deaths <70 by intervention and sex
tab_u70_int_sex <- dt[age_group == "<70",
  .(deaths_u70 = sum(dead, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = .(intervention, sex)
][order(intervention, sex)]

# 2) Top 30 most populous countries: <70 deaths delayed + share of delayed deaths that are <70
# pick top 30 by population (BAU, year 2025), using one cause to avoid double counting pop
pop_cause <- dt[!is.na(cause), unique(cause)][1]

pop_2025 <- dt[intervention == "b.a.u" & year == 2025 & cause == pop_cause,
  .(population = sum(pop, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = .(location)
][order(-population)]

top30_locs <- pop_2025[1:30, location]

base_loc_age <- dt[intervention == "b.a.u",
  .(deaths_base = sum(dead, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = .(location, age_group)
]

all_loc_age <- dt[intervention == "All Interventions",
  .(deaths_allint = sum(dead, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = .(location, age_group)
]

loc_age <- merge(all_loc_age, base_loc_age,
  by = c("location", "age_group"),
  all.x = TRUE
)

loc_age[, deaths_delayed := deaths_base - deaths_allint]

tab_top30_u70_delayed <- loc_age[location %in% top30_locs,
  .(
    deaths_delayed_u70   = sum(deaths_delayed[age_group == "<70"], na.rm = TRUE),
    deaths_delayed_total = sum(deaths_delayed, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = .(location)
]

tab_top30_u70_delayed[, prop_delayed_u70 :=
  fifelse(deaths_delayed_total == 0, NA_real_, deaths_delayed_u70 / deaths_delayed_total)
]

tab_top30_u70_delayed <- merge(
  tab_top30_u70_delayed,
  pop_2025[, .(location, population)],
  by = "location",
  all.x = TRUE
)[order(-population)]

tab_top30_u70_delayed_out <- tab_top30_u70_delayed[, .(
  location,
  deaths_delayed_u70,      # both sexes combined, All Interventions vs BAU
  prop_delayed_u70         # share of total delayed deaths that are <70
)]

# --- Add sheets (safe: overwrite if sheet already exists) ---
add_or_replace_sheet <- function(wb, sheet_name) {
  if (sheet_name %in% names(wb)) removeWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
  addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
}

add_or_replace_sheet(wb, "U70_by_Int_Sex")
writeData(wb, "U70_by_Int_Sex", tab_u70_int_sex)

add_or_replace_sheet(wb, "Top30_U70_Delayed")
writeData(wb, "Top30_U70_Delayed", tab_top30_u70_delayed_out)

# Save workbook
saveWorkbook(wb, "UWRTSL-100MLives_Output.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
```



<!-- # Code -->

<!-- ```{r ref.label=knitr::all_labels(), echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE} -->
<!-- ``` -->
